title: Add a way to enable builtkit in podman compose
body:
### Feature request description

* Any recent Containerfile uses buildkit features (secrets, additional mounts, ...).
* `podman compose` forcefully sets `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0` https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/1e53cacb08373b24dcc5c64ecb0cddfb405ec3eb/cmd/podman/compose.go#L164
* `podman compose` is still the best tool for local development / debugging honestly

I would like for a way to tell `podman compose` "I know what I'm doing, let me use buildkit features, I promise I'll fix it myself if it breaks".

### Suggest potential solution

There are multiple solutions possible:
1. Don't set `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0` if there is already `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1`
2. Add a `PODMAN_COMPOSE_BUILDKIT` env var

I guess in any case a warning should be printed like: "podman buildkit support is incomplete, see issue #xyz"

### Have you considered any alternatives?

This blog post: <https://emersion.fr/blog/2025/using-podman-compose-and-buildkit/>
proposes 2 solutions:
* use `docker compose` with `podman` backend
* export `bake.json` using `buildx` and pass it through [bakah](https://github.com/emersion/bakah) which in turns calls `buildah`

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: `podman compose` is sort of a pass-through command.  can you clarify for me if you have `podman-compose` installed or `docker-compose` installed?  If the answer is `podman-compose`, then I'm not certain how much help we can be because that project is not maintained or developed by us.  lets also see what the team has to say.

I use `podman compose` with the `docker-compose` provider. The issue is that `podman compose` doesn't let me use buildkit at all (it forces `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0`).

I understand that podman doesn't fully support buildkit commands coming from `docker-compose` but it should let me decide for myself to try or not.

> but it should let me decide for myself to try or not

I'm not sure I agree with this BUT let's ask the original author.  @vrothberg it looks like you [set the environment variable](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/compose.go#L164), would you see any harm in having a conditional in there that if the variable is already set, we honor that?  It might be a simple PR that @awoimbee could cobble up to do local testing first too.
@baude This requires us to support the BuildKit GRPC API - which we have discussed previously, and would be a very large time investment.
i think you are misreading the ask.  the ask is to alter the environment variable not do all of buildx at least the way I read it.

@baude That's not how it works. If we turn off that environment variable, Docker starts speaking the BuildKit GRPC API to us. There is no "degrees of support" given that - either we speak the API, or we don't.

Oh so that's off the table :(

The other solutions to get full compose build compatibility are then:
* use bake files (`docker buildx bake`): bake files don't support everything (eg secrets) so nope
* Use `docker` CLI with the podman socket:
  ```sh
  docker context create podman --docker host=unix://$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock
  docker context use podman
  ```
  I guess this is OK, it seems to provide full compatibility ? Is it something we want to document ?

> Oh so that's off the table :(
> 
> The other solutions to get full compose build compatibility are then:
> 
>     * use bake files (`docker buildx bake`): bake files don't support everything (eg secrets) so nope
> 
>     * Use `docker` CLI with the podman socket:
>       docker context create podman --docker host=unix://$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock
>       docker context use podman
>           
>         
>       I guess this is OK, it seems to provide full compatibility ? Is it something we want to document ?

hi, how can I use this solution? I use that `docker context` but still have warning messsage when building image, `WARN[0000] Docker Compose is configured to build using Bake, but buildkit isn't enabled `

I wanted to try out podman, unfortunately hit the same issue. Since its been almost a year without any progress looks like its not usable for setups with secrets.

```
the classic builder doesn't support secrets, set DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 to use BuildKit
Error: executing /usr/local/bin/docker-compose build: exit status 1
error: Recipe `build` failed on line 107 with exit code 1
error: Recipe `conditional-build` failed with exit code 1
```
Work on supporting the LLB API is underway and tracked at https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6732
